record.field:
# LUL TODO check this comment # The context key 'last_sync_hours' allows specifying the minimum delay between consecutive syncs. # Indeed, in case there are many users / events to sync, the cron might time out. In this case, the # solution is to force the last_sync_hours to the expected synchronization interval expected. This # will avoid the synchronization of users that succeeded in the previous failing cron. today = datetime.date.today() date_last_week = today + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=-7)
employee_ids = model.search([('exclude_from_timesheet_quality_control', '=', False)]) timesheet_ids = env['account.analytic.line'].search([('date', '>=', date_last_week), ('date', '<', today)]) cron_id = env.ref('cap_quality_issue_log.ir_cron_employee_timesheet_log') issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('ir_cron_id', '=', cron_id.id)]) for employee in employee_ids: holidays_ids = env['resource.calendar.leaves'].search([('resource_id', '=', False), ('calendar_id', '=', employee.resource_calendar_id.id), ('date_from', '<', today), ('date_from', '>=', date_last_week)]) crnt_emp_timesheet_ids = timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.employee_id.id == employee.id) leave_ids = env['hr.leave'].search([('employee_id', '=', employee.id), ('state', '=', 'validate'), ('date_from', '>=', date_last_week), ('date_to', '<=', today)]) date_to_check = date_last_week while date_to_check < today: if date_to_check.weekday() in [0, 1, 2, 3, 4]: holiday_id = holidays_ids.filtered(lambda holiday: holiday.date_from.date() >= date_to_check and holiday.date_to.date() <= date_to_check) leave_id = leave_ids.filtered(lambda leave: leave.request_date_from >= date_to_check and leave.request_date_to <= date_to_check) same_day_timesheet_ids = timesheet_ids.filtered(lambda timesheet: timesheet.date == date_to_check and timesheet.employee_id.id == employee.id) if not holiday_id: if not leave_id or (leave_id and leave_id.request_unit_half): if not same_day_timesheet_ids or (same_day_timesheet_ids and sum(tm.unit_amount for tm in same_day_timesheet_ids) < 1): issue_log_ids = env['quality.issue.log'].search([('logged_date', '=', date_to_check), ('employee_id', '=', employee.id), ('quality_issue_type', '=', issue_type.id)]) if not issue_log_ids: vals = {'logged_date': date_to_check, 'employee_id': employee.id, 'description': 'There is no timesheet logged.', 'score_impact': issue_type.score_impact, 'quality_issue_type': issue_type.id } env['quality.issue.log'].create(vals) date_to_check = date_to_check + dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta(days=+1)

#Retrieve the automation rule automation_id = env.ref('cap_quality_issue_log.base_automation_customer_lost') #Retrieve the quality issue type linked to the automation rule issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('base_automation_id', '=', automation_id.id)]) #Iterate over affected customer (partner) records for rec in records: #Retrieve active projects linked to the customer with assigned project managers project_ids = env['project.project'].search([('partner_id', '=', rec.id), ('user_id', '!=', False)]) #Process each relevant project for project_id in project_ids: domain = [('project_id', '=', project_id.id)] msg = '{} Customer Lost'.format(str(project_id.name)) type = 'penalty' #Handle project manager role if project_id.user_id: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(project_id.user_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_project_manager', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log if applicable issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, project_id, type) #Handle business analyst roles if project_id.business_analyst_ids: for business_analyst_id in project_id.business_analyst_ids: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(business_analyst_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_business_analyst', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log if applicable issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, project_id, type) #Handle developer roles if project_id.developers_ids: for developers_id in project_id.developers_ids: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(developers_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_developer', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log if applicable issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, project_id, type) #Handle configurator roles if project_id.configurators_ids: for configurators_id in project_id.configurators_ids: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(configurators_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_configurator', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log if applicable issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, project_id, type) #Handle architect roles if project_id.architect_ids: for architect_id in project_id.architect_ids: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(architect_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_architect', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log if applicable issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, project_id, type)

#Retrieve the automation rule automation_id = env.ref('cap_quality_issue_log.base_automation_go_live_date_change_request') #Retrieve the quality issue type linked to the automation rule issue_type = env['quality.issue.type'].search([('base_automation_id', '=', automation_id.id)]) #Retrieve the quality category linked to the issue type quality_category = issue_type.quality_category #Iterate over the triggered records for rec in records: #Validate conditions for approved late go-live date change request if rec.state == 'direction_approved' and rec.reason == 'in_late' and rec.project_id.user_id and rec.project_id.on_hold_reason == False and rec.project_id.exclude_for_go_live == False and (rec.project_id.project_status_id == False or rec.project_id.project_status_id.code not in ['dead', 'duplicate', 'internal', 'internal_p2p3']): domain = [('project_id', '=', rec.project_id.id), ('logged_date', '=', datetime.date.today())] msg = 'Go live date change request for : {} is approved.'.format(rec.project_id.name) type = 'penalty' #Process issue logs based on configured planning roles if quality_category.role_ids: for role_id in quality_category.role_ids: #Validate planning role and create issue log if applicable issue_type.check_planning_role(rec.project_id, role_id, domain, msg, type, is_from_auto=True) #Fallback to project manager role if no roles are configured else: employee_id = issue_type.find_employee_based_on_company(rec.project_id.user_id) role_id = env['planning.role'].search([('is_project_manager', '=', True)]) #Search and create quality issue log issue_type.search_and_create_issue_log(domain, employee_id, role_id, msg, rec.project_id, type, is_from_auto=True)
